clues.clue: 'I'm Lovin' It,' launched in 2003, is the slogan of this golden-arches chain.
'Eat Fresh' was the long-time motto of this footlong-sandwich chain.
'Finger Lickin' Good' is the classic line of this fried-chicken chain.
'Think Outside the Bun' pitched this Tex-Mex fast-food chain.
'Quality Is Our Recipe' is the official slogan of this square-patty chain.
This NFL team's name nods to Green Bay's meatpacking heritage.
This Boston NBA franchise, with a leprechaun logo, has won 17 titles.
This Pittsburgh NFL team is named for the city's dominant industry.
This New York MLB club, the 'Bronx Bombers,' owns 27 championships.
The NBA Lakers kept their watery name after moving from this lake-dotted state.
In this island game, players gather brick, wood, sheep, wheat and ore to build settlements.
In this cooperative game, players race to cure four diseases sweeping the globe.
In this game, players collect colored cards to claim cross-country rail routes.
This 2000 tile-laying game about a fortified French town won the Spiel des Jahres.
Germany's top board-game prize, 'Spiel des Jahres,' translates to 'game of the' this.
These frosted, shredded-wheat pillows come in a 'bite size' version.
These sugar-dusted square pieces are hawked by mischievous cartoon bakers.
This General Mills mix of toasted oats and marshmallows debuted in 1964.
This chocolate cereal turns milk brown and is fronted by a friendly vampire count.
This Kellogg's cereal of glazed, puffed corn was marketed for decades as 'Sugar Pops.'
The Sun's visible, light-emitting surface layer goes by this name.
The Moon always shows Earth one face because of this 1:1 spin-orbit phenomenon.
During a total solar eclipse, the Sun's faint outer atmosphere appears as this pearly halo.
Sun-like stars fuse hydrogen into helium mainly through this dominant reaction sequence.
This roughly 18-year, 11-day period, after which eclipse geometry nearly repeats, bears this name.

final.clue: The 1939 film 'The Wizard of Oz' ends with Dorothy repeating 'there's no place like' this.
